subject to qualifying the CPT. Candidates who clear the CPT but score lower in CSE-2024 will rank below those with higher CSE-2024 scores
making UPSC marks the decisive factor.

subject to a minimum of five). Shortlisting is based on total marks obtained in UPSC CSE-2024 Mains plus Personality Test. content_sections.heading: CSIR ASO Recruitment 2026 - Notification Out
content_sections.text: CSIR Headquarters has released the official recruitment notification for the post of Assistant Section Officer across three cadres - General, Finance & Accounts, and Stores & Purchase. This is a unique opportunity designed exclusively for UPSC CSE-2024 aspirants who cleared various stages but were not finally recommended for IAS/IPS or allied services. Eligible candidates can apply online through the CSIR recruitment portal before the deadline.

content_sections.heading: CSIR ASO Recruitment 2026 - Overview
content_sections.text: Daily Current Affairs 23 June 2026: Download Free PDF Now

Council of Scientific & Industrial Research (CSIR), New Delhi
Non-recommended candidates of UPSC CSE-2024 with University degree

content_sections.heading: CSIR ASO Recruitment 2026 - Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates planning to apply for CSIR ASO Recruitment 2026 should mark these dates carefully. The application portal opened on 23 June 2026 and will close on 13 July 2026 at 5:00 PM. Both the application submission and fee payment deadlines fall on the same date, so candidates should avoid waiting until the last day.

content_sections.heading: CSIR ASO Vacancy 2026 - Post-wise & Category-wise Breakdown
content_sections.text: CSIR has advertised 100 ASO posts across three specialised cadres. All three posts share the same pay scale at Pay Level 7, Cell 1. Category-wise distribution ensures representation across UR, SC, ST, OBC (NCL), and EWS groups. Candidates should note that 7 of these 100 posts (including 2 backlog) are reserved for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) with not less than 40% of relevant disability. Note on backlog vacancies: In the ASO (Finance & Accounts) category, 1 vacancy under ST is a Backlog Vacancy, and 2 of the 6 OBC vacancies are Backlog Vacancies.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria - Who Can Apply for CSIR ASO 2026?
content_sections.text: The eligibility for CSIR ASO Recruitment 2026 is highly specific and differs from standard government job notifications. Only candidates who appeared in UPSC's Civil Services Examination 2024 but were not finally recommended for any service - and whose details appear on the UPSC Pratibha Setu Portal - are eligible. General graduates not associated with CSE-2024 are not eligible for this recruitment. Candidates must hold a University degree and must be non-recommended candidates of UPSC Civil Services Examination 2024 (CSE-2024) , as per details available on the UPSC Pratibha Setu Portal. There is no branch or subject restriction on the degree - any graduate from a recognised university qualifies on academic grounds, provided the CSE-2024 condition is met. Candidates wondering about the age limit for CSIR ASO 2026 should note that the upper age limit is 33 years as on the closing date of online application. Age relaxation is available for reserved categories as listed below.

rows.age_relaxation: 3 years after deducting actual military service from age on closing date

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: The application fee for CSIR ASO 2026 is payable online through SBI Collect. Women candidates, SC, ST, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen candidates are fully exempted from paying any fee. Fee-paying candidates must upload the e-Receipt in the online application before the submission deadline.

content_sections.heading: CSIR ASO Selection Process 2026
content_sections.text: The CSIR ASO 2026 selection process involves two key stages - shortlisting based on CSE-2024 marks, followed by a Computer Proficiency Test (CPT). The final merit is determined by CSE-2024 total marks (Mains + Personality Test), subject to qualifying the CPT. Candidates who clear the CPT but score lower in CSE-2024 will rank below those with higher CSE-2024 scores, making UPSC marks the decisive factor. Application Screening: Applications are screened and candidates are shortlisted for CPT in the ratio of 1:5 (i.e., 5 times total vacancies per category, subject to a minimum of five). Shortlisting is based on total marks obtained in UPSC CSE-2024 Mains plus Personality Test. Computer Proficiency Test (CPT): Shortlisted candidates appear for a qualifying CPT of 100 marks and 1-hour duration. Qualifying the CPT is mandatory for further consideration. Final Merit List: Prepared based on total CSE-2024 marks (Mains + Personality Test), subject to qualifying the CPT with the minimum threshold marks prescribed by CSIR. Cadre Allocation: A single merit list is drawn. CSIR allocates cadre (General / Finance & Accounts / Stores & Purchase) based on merit and the candidate's choice submitted in the online application. Cadre once allotted is final and unchangeable. Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates are called to produce original documents for verification. In case of a tie in scores, CSIR resolves it in the following order of precedence: Higher marks in Mains Examination of CSE-2024 Date of Birth - older candidate placed higher Earlier acquisition of Essential Degree Alphabetical order of first names

content_sections.heading: Computer Proficiency Test (CPT) - Pattern & Minimum Qualifying Marks
content_sections.text: The Computer Proficiency Test for CSIR ASO 2026 is qualifying in nature and carries 100 marks with a duration of 1 hour. Candidates who fail to secure the prescribed minimum threshold marks will be eliminated from further consideration regardless of their CSE-2024 scores. The CPT will be held tentatively in Delhi, though CSIR reserves the right to conduct it in other cities as well. The CPT will assess proficiency in Word Processing (typing and document exercises), Spreadsheet preparation, and PowerPoint Presentation - skills essential to day-to-day office functioning. For PwBD candidates, Govt. of India / Staff Selection Commission (SSC) rules on CPT shall apply.

content_sections.heading: CSIR ASO Salary & Benefits 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates selected as Assistant Section Officer in CSIR will be placed at Pay Level 7, Cell 1 of the 7th Pay Commission Pay Matrix, with a starting basic pay of ₹44,900 per month scaling up to ₹1,42,400. Beyond the base salary, CSIR employees receive a comprehensive benefits package aligned with Central Government norms. Officers appointed will be liable to serve anywhere in India across any of CSIR's 37 national laboratories, institutes, extension centres, or CSIR Headquarters at Anusandhan Bhawan, New Delhi. content_sections.heading: Apply Online - How to Submit the CSIR ASO Application Form 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates must apply online only through the official CSIR websites. No offline, postal, or email-based applications will be accepted. The online portal is available via Google Chrome (recommended) and requires a valid, active email ID throughout the recruitment process. Visit https://www.csir.res.in or https://recruitment.csir.res.in . Click "Apply Online" and register using your UPSC Roll Number , email address, and mobile number. A User ID and password will be sent to your registered email and mobile. Log in with the received credentials and complete the Profile Page (Gender, Date of Birth, Category, PwBD status, Ex-Serviceman status, CSIR employee status etc.). Once locked, profile details cannot be modified. Fill in the 6-tab application form: Apply → Personal Details → Education → Experience → Additional Details → Uploads → Preview & Submit. Upload all required scanned documents (photographs, signature, certificates) as per specifications. Preview the complete form and click Submit. An Application Number will be generated. If fee is applicable, pay ₹500 via SBI Collect (UPI, Debit Card, Net Banking). Download the e-Receipt and upload it in the application before the deadline. Retain a printout of the final submitted form for future reference.

content_sections.heading: Troubleshooting & Common Mistakes to Avoid
content_sections.text: The CSIR ASO application process has several technical and eligibility-specific requirements that can trip up even well-prepared candidates. The mistakes below are the most commonly observed reasons for application rejection or disqualification - review each carefully before submitting. Not appearing on UPSC Pratibha Setu Portal: Eligibility is strictly tied to being listed on the UPSC Pratibha Setu Portal as a non-recommended CSE-2024 candidate. If your name does not appear there, you are not eligible - no exceptions. Locking profile with incorrect data: Once the profile page is locked on the portal, details like name, category, and date of birth cannot be changed. Any discrepancy found at document verification will result in cancellation of candidature. Uploading fee receipt after deadline: Even if you pay the fee before 13 July 2026, failing to upload the SBI Collect e-Receipt within the application portal by 5:00 PM on 13 July 2026 means your application will not be treated as complete. Using a degree from an unrecognised institution: The qualifying degree must be from a University/Institution/Board recognised by the Government of India. The final result must have been declared on or before the closing date of online application. Applying with a CGPA without converting to percentage: Universities awarding CGPA/SGPA grades must have the percentage calculated as per the formula prescribed by their University/Institute before filling the application. Government employees applying without NOC: Applicants currently employed in Government Departments must produce a valid 'No Objection Certificate' from their employer at the time of document verification. An undertaking to resign or experience certificate will not be accepted in lieu of NOC. Canvassing in any form: Canvassing or attempting to influence the selection through any means is treated as disqualification for the post and can lead to debarment from future CSIR recruitments. Inactive email ID: All communications, including interview/CPT call letters, are sent only by email. A non-functional or frequently unchecked email ID (including spam/junk folders) can cause candidates to miss critical updates.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in CSIR Assistant Section Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: CSIR has announced 100 vacancies for the post of Assistant Section Officer under Advertisement No. E-I/RC/2026/2. These include 61 posts for ASO (General), 18 for ASO (Finance & Accounts), and 21 for ASO (Stores & Purchase). Additionally, 7 posts out of the 100 are reserved for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities.

faq.question: Q2. Who is eligible to apply for CSIR ASO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Only candidates who are non-recommended candidates of UPSC Civil Services Examination 2024 (CSE-2024) and hold a University degree are eligible. Candidates must appear on the UPSC Pratibha Setu Portal. General graduates who did not appear in CSE-2024 are not eligible for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply for CSIR ASO 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for both online application submission and fee payment is 13 July 2026 at 05:00 PM. Applications must be submitted through https://www.csir.res.in or https://recruitment.csir.res.in.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary for CSIR Assistant Section Officer?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will be appointed at Pay Level 7, Cell 1 of the 7th CPC Pay Matrix, with a basic pay range of ₹44,900 to ₹1,42,400 per month, along with DA, HRA, Transport Allowance, and other Central Government service benefits.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for CSIR ASO 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates from UR, OBC (NCL), and EWS categories must pay ₹500 via SBI Collect. Women, SC, ST, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen candidates are exempted from paying any application fee.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for CSIR ASO Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection involves three stages: (1) shortlisting based on CSE-2024 Mains + Personality Test marks in a 1:5 ratio per category; (2) a qualifying Computer Proficiency Test (CPT) of 100 marks and 1-hour duration; and (3) final merit prepared on the basis of CSE-2024 total marks, subject to clearing CPT minimum threshold marks.

faq.question: Q7. What is the Computer Proficiency Test (CPT) for CSIR ASO?
faq.answer: The CPT is a 100-mark, 1-hour qualifying test covering Word Processing, Spreadsheet preparation, and PowerPoint Presentation. Minimum qualifying marks range from 30 (PwBD categories) to 40 (UR category) out of 100. The CPT is tentatively scheduled to be held in Delhi.

faq.question: Q8. What is the age limit for CSIR ASO 2026?
faq.answer: The upper age limit is 33 years as on 13 July 2026 (the closing date). Age relaxation is available: 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (NCL), 10 years for PwBD (UR), 15 years for PwBD (SC/ST), 13 years for PwBD (OBC), and 5 years for CSIR departmental candidates.

faq.question: Q9. How will cadre be allotted - General, Finance & Accounts, or Stores & Purchase?
faq.answer: CSIR will prepare a single merit list. Cadre allocation (General / Finance & Accounts / Stores & Purchase) will be decided based on merit and the candidate's preferred cadre choice submitted in the online application form. Once allotted, the cadre is final and cannot be changed.

faq.question: Q10. What documents are required at the time of CSIR ASO document verification?
faq.answer: Candidates must carry original documents including proof of date of birth, educational qualification certificates (10th onwards), CSE-2024 mark sheets (Prelims and Mains + Personality Test), caste/community certificate (if applicable), PwBD certificate (if applicable), employment certificate, and a No Objection Certificate from employer (if currently in government service). Self-attested copies of all uploaded documents must also be brought.

faq.question: How many vacancies are there in CSIR Assistant Section Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: CSIR has announced 100 vacancies for the post of Assistant Section Officer under Advertisement No. E-I/RC/2026/2.
